response.justification: While the candidate exceeds the 15-year experience requirement and demonstrates strong technical fundamentals across multiple domains, they fall short of the core specialization required for this director-level AI strategy role. AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq is recent (14 months) and represents infrastructure/tooling contribution rather than demonstrated deep expertise or recognized leadership in AI/ML. The role explicitly requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI,' which is not evident. The candidate lacks an advanced degree in AI and shows limited strategic leadership experience beyond technical execution.
strengths: Exceeds experience requirements with 19 years spanning web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and infrastructure engineering across diverse technical domains
Demonstrates strong engineering craftsmanship with consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and software quality practices throughout career
Proven entrepreneurial initiative with GISCollective founding and contributions to multiple startups, plus community engagement through workshops and open-source projects
concerns: AI/ML expertise is insufficient and recent—current MCP and AI-agent infrastructure work (14 months) lacks depth, duration, and demonstrated products or platforms that would establish recognized AI authority

Limited evidence of strategic leadership required for director-level role—background is primarily technical individual contributor without clear record of identifying strategic opportunities, advising customers on cutting-edge technology, or driving platform vision

Missing preferred qualification of Master's or PhD in AI-related field; career trajectory shows generalist full-stack engineer recently pivoting toward AI infrastructure rather than deep specialist grooming

key_factors.factor: Limited strategic leadership and advisory experience
Strong technical breadth and engineering fundamentals
